blocks.task: TPC-H failure analysis: recursive CTE arithmetic in WHERE clause. Parser bug — rc.rkey + 1 in WHERE fails. Investigate and plan fix.
blocks.summary: Found the bug: parseComparison() line 1185 calls parsePrimary() for the RHS of comparison operators (=, <, >, etc.), but should call parsePrimaryWithConcat() to handle arithmetic. x = y + 1 parses RHS as just y, then + 1 is unexpected. Same bug in BETWEEN, LIKE, NOT IN value lists. Fix: change parsePrimary() to parsePrimaryWithConcat() in all RHS positions within parseComparison.
blocks.details: Found the bug: parseComparison() line 1185 calls parsePrimary() for the RHS of comparison operators (=, <, >, etc.), but should call parsePrimaryWithConcat() to handle arithmetic. x = y + 1 parses RHS as just y, then + 1 is unexpected. Same bug in BETWEEN, LIKE, NOT IN value lists. Fix: change parsePrimary() to parsePrimaryWithConcat() in all RHS positions within parseComparison.

blocks.task: Depth pivot: stress-test HenryDB's MVCC + SSI with concurrent transaction scenarios. Find bugs in the isolation guarantees.
blocks.summary: FOUND BUG: PK violation under SSI. When T-A updates+commits a row, T-B (started before commit) can update the same row and create a duplicate PK. The UPDATE inserts a new heap entry instead of detecting the committed write conflict. This is a real MVCC bug — the update path does not check if the target row was modified by another committed transaction since B s snapshot.

Also verified write skew detection still works independently.

blocks.task: Explore RISC-V emulator current state: test coverage, architecture, what register allocator currently does, what liveness analysis would improve.
blocks.summary: RISC-V codegen has 1940 LOC. Current register allocator is linear assignment (s1, s2, s3...) without liveness analysis. Falls back to stack when 11 callee-saved regs exhausted. No dead variable reclamation. A liveness-based allocator (CFG → liveness → interference → coloring) would reduce spills for functions with many variables but few simultaneously live. Good algorithmic project for tomorrow.

blocks.task: Investigate SSI false positive — deferred read recording. What lines in transactional-db.js record reads? Can we defer to post-WHERE?
blocks.summary: SSI false positive for UPDATEs is ALREADY mitigated! Lines 960-967 in transactional-db.js set _suppressSsiReads=true during UPDATE execution, preventing the SeqScan from recording reads for non-matching rows. Only the actual modified rows get conflict-tracked (via the delete interceptor at line 485). The remaining SSI false positives are for SELECT scans that read broadly.

blocks.task: Review the MVCC findByPK optimization issue from TODO. What would it take to optimize PK lookups to avoid full scan?
blocks.summary: MVCC findByPK optimization: _tryIndexScan exists and works for equality lookups on indexed columns. The transactional layer intercepts heap operations but NOT index lookups. The remaining issue: index scan may return rows that are MVCC-invisible (deleted by concurrent tx or not yet visible). The fix: add MVCC visibility filtering after index lookup. Currently, the version map check happens in the heap scan interceptor but not in the index scan path.

blocks.task: Investigate SMT theory propagation bug: x > 10 AND x < 5 returns SAT. Read smt.cjs to understand the theory solver integration.
blocks.summary: FOUND ROOT CAUSE: _processAssertion handles <= and >= but NOT < or > (strict inequalities). When (> x 10) is asserted, it falls through without being processed — the assertion is silently ignored. Fix: add < and > cases, convert to weak inequalities for integers: x > 10 → x >= 11, x < 5 → x <= 4. This is a simple 8-line fix (add two else-if branches).

blocks.task: Investigate monkey-lang to RISC-V compilation pipeline: how does the codegen connect, what optimization passes exist, where does register allocation fit.
blocks.summary: Monkey→RISC-V pipeline: AST → RiscVCodeGen (stack-based code gen) → RISC-V assembly → Assembler → Machine code → CPU emulator. Current register allocator: linear assignment of s1-s11 without deallocation. Falls back to stack after 11 vars. Liveness improvement: track last-use, reuse dead registers. For 20-var function with max 5 live: 0 spills vs 9 currently. Key implementation: compute last-use for each variable, free register after last use, allocate from freed pool first.

blocks.task: Write a scratch note analyzing the Monkey-lang optimization passes: const-prop, DCE, escape analysis, inline caching. Which passes interact with codegen?
blocks.summary: Monkey-lang optimization pipeline: Parse→AST→CFG→SCCP→DCE→Escape Analysis→Bytecode (with hidden classes for IC). Four major optimization passes: CFG (365 LOC), SCCP (208), DCE (228), Escape Analysis (267). Hidden classes (260 LOC) for V8-style inline caching. Register allocation for RISC-V codegen would integrate with escape analysis: non-escaping vars → register candidates. The CFG builder could provide the basic blocks needed for liveness analysis.

blocks.task: Design flash attention for neural-net project: what would it require? How to implement O(N) memory attention without materializing the full NxN attention matrix.
blocks.summary: Flash attention design: tiled computation with online softmax trick. Process Q×K in blocks, maintain running max/sum statistics, never materialize full N×N matrix. O(N) memory vs O(N²). For JS: benefit is memory allocation and cache locality (computation still O(N²)). ~80 LOC implementation. Key algorithm: online softmax with rescaling. Would make a good addition to attention.js.

blocks.task: Compare HenryDB compiled query engine vs Volcano engine: what does each handle, where do they overlap, could they be unified?
blocks.summary: HenryDB has THREE execution engines: (1) db.js interpreter (most general, now with Volcano for joins), (2) Volcano engine (iterator-based, all operators, cost model), (3) Compiled query engine (JIT/vectorized, highest perf). compiled-query.js (944 LOC) bridges planner with pipeline JIT compiler. 16 compiled-vs-volcano tests pass. Could be unified: use Volcano planner output as input to JIT compilation. The compiled engine already imports from volcano.js.

blocks.task: Investigate HenryDB query planner (planner.js) vs volcano-planner.js: how do they differ? Can they be merged?
blocks.summary: Two planners: planner.js (971 LOC, full cost-based DP optimizer with histograms) and volcano-planner.js (740 LOC, heuristic plan builder with basic costs). They could be merged: use planner.js for optimization decisions (join order, strategy selection), output Volcano iterator trees. This would give sophisticated optimization + clean iterator execution. The compiled-query.js already does this partially — it uses planner.js output.
